name: agileagentcanvas
description: Visual AI-assisted product development with BMAD methodology - Design epics, stories, and requirements collaboratively with interactive AI chat workflows

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_read_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads a file from the BMAD framework folder or the project output folder. Use this to read agent definitions, workflow steps, schemas, checklists, and any other BMAD framework files.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to read

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_list_directory
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists the contents of a directory inside the BMAD framework folder. Use this to discover available workflows, agents, schemas, and steps.

path.description: Absolute path to the directory to list

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_update_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Saves changes to a BMAD artifact (vision, epic, story, requirement, etc.) in the project. Call this when you have completed refining an artifact and are ready to persist the changes.

type.description: Artifact type: vision, epic, story, requirement, requirements

id.description: Artifact ID, e.g. EPIC-1, STORY-1-1, vision-1, FR-1

changes.description: The fields to update on the artifact, as a JSON object conforming to the artifact schema

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_graph_query
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a natural-language question against the graphify knowledge graph of the codebase. Use this to understand code structure, trace connections between components, or surface design rationale. Requires graphify-out/graph.json to exist.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_graph_path
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Find the shortest path between two nodes in the graphify knowledge graph. Use this to trace dependencies or understand how two components relate. Requires graphify-out/graph.json.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_read_jira
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads epics and stories from the user's Jira Cloud project via the REST API. Use this when the user asks about their Jira board, wants to see Jira issues, or asks about epics/stories in Jira. Requires Jira to be configured in VS Code Settings (agileagentcanvas.jira.*).

epicKey.description: Epic issue key, e.g. PROJ-42 (for list_stories)

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_codeburn_report
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads AI coding cost and token usage from Codeburn. Use when the user asks about AI spend, token usage, costs, budget, or model pricing. Codeburn reads session data directly from disk (no API keys).

period.description: Time period: today, 7days, 30days, or omit for current status

action.description: summary (default) for cost overview, models for per-model breakdown

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_write_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Write a file (BMAD artifact or generic). Auto-handles .md/.json dual format. Required for any file creation. NEVER use shell commands or write temp scripts to create files — call this tool instead.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to write

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_sync_story_status
languageModelTools.displayName: Agile Agent Canvas Sync Story Status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Atomically update a story's status across ALL tracker files (story.md, epic.md, sprint-status.yaml, etc.) in one call. Use this when changing a story's lifecycle state — never read+modify+write the files manually.

status.description: New lifecycle status for the story

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_sync_epic_status
languageModelTools.displayName: Agile Agent Canvas Sync Epic Status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Atomically update an epic's status across ALL tracker files. Use instead of manual multi-file edits.

status.description: New lifecycle status for the epic

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_graph_community
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the wiki summary for a code community (e.g. 'authentication', 'payments') from the graphify knowledge graph. Use BEFORE writing code in an unfamiliar module to learn the architecture. Requires graphify-out/graph.json.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_repair_json
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Repair malformed JSON against a BMAD schema. Auto-fills missing required fields, coerces type mismatches (string↔number↔boolean), fuzzy-matches invalid enum values (e.g. 'urgent' → 'P0', 'in progress' → 'in-progress'), clamps numeric ranges, picks the best oneOf branch, strips disallowed properties. Use this when an agileagentcanvas_update_artifact call was REJECTED with validation errors (feed it the same changes object — it will return a fixed version), or when the LLM produced incomplete JSON for any BMAD artifact type. Returns { ok, changed, data, repairs, repairCount }. NEVER write your own JSON repair code; always call this tool instead.

data.description: The malformed JSON to repair. Pass the LLM output as-is.

schemaName.description: BMAD schema name. Examples: story, prd, architecture, requirement, test-case, sprint-status, risk. The schemaName input has an `enum` constraint listing all valid types.

strict.description: If true, only succeeds when no repairs were needed. Default false — best-effort repair is usually what you want.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_frontmatter_extract
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Extract YAML frontmatter from a markdown file as JSON. ALWAYS use this instead of writing a YAML parser or calling Python — saves tokens and avoids parse errors. Returns { frontmatter, body }.

path.description: Absolute path to the .md file under allowed roots.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_yaml_to_json
languageModelTools.displayName: Agile Agent Canvas YAML to JSON
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Convert a YAML string to a JSON object. Use when reading BMAD artifacts stored as .yaml files, or when the LLM emits YAML and you need JSON. NEVER write a YAML parser inline.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_json_diff
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Compute a structured diff between two JSON objects. Returns a patch array and a summary { added, removed, modified }. Use instead of reading two files and comparing them in-context — saves ~400 tokens per diff.

format.description: Output format. "summary" is fastest; "unified" mimics git diff.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_json_merge
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Deep-merge two JSON objects with a configurable strategy. Use instead of manually combining JSON in-context. Strategies: 'deep' (recursive), 'shallow' (top-level only), 'right-authoritative' (right wins on conflict), 'array-replace' (arrays overwritten, not concatenated).

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_artifact_query
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Query the artifact store with filter criteria (type, status, epicId, priority, etc.) and return matching artifacts. Use this instead of reading the entire artifact directory and filtering in context — saves hundreds of tokens per query.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_workflow_resolve_vars
languageModelTools.displayName: Agile Agent Canvas Workflow Resolve Vars
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Resolve {{variable}} placeholders in a BMAD workflow template string. Variables can come from (a) the provided `vars` object, (b) artifact fields referenced as {{artifact.field}}, or (c) environment / config. Use this instead of writing your own regex replacement.

template.description: The workflow template string containing {{var}} placeholders

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_types_from_schema
languageModelTools.displayName: Agile Agent Canvas Types From Schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generate TypeScript interface declarations from a JSON schema object. Handles required/optional, enums, nested objects, and arrays. Use this instead of writing the interfaces by hand from a schema.

schema.description: The JSON schema object (output of `agileagentcanvas_repair_json` or read from `resources/_aac/schemas/`)

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_schema_from_json
languageModelTools.displayName: Agile Agent Canvas Schema From JSON
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Infer a JSON schema from one or more sample JSON objects. The more samples provided, the more accurate the schema. Use this when you have JSON output but no schema — avoids hand-rolling a schema.

languageModelTools.name: agileagentcanvas_codebase_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the workspace for symbol definitions, references, or text matches. Use this instead of running `grep` via shell — returns structured results with file paths, line numbers, and context. Backed by ripgrep with built-in ignore (.gitignore, node_modules, dist).

query.description: Search query — symbol name, function call, or text pattern. Supports ripgrep regex.

kind.description: Search kind. 'definition' finds where symbols are defined (e.g. `function foo`, `class Bar`); 'reference' finds all uses; 'text' is plain ripgrep text search. Default: 'text'.

filePattern.description: Glob pattern to scope the search (e.g. '**/*.ts')

chatParticipants.name: agileagentcanvas
chatParticipants.description: AI Business Analyst for product development - helps design epics, stories, and requirements

commands.description: Convert markdown artifacts to structured JSON format

commands.name: status

commands.description: Sprint planning from epics or check sprint status

commands.description: Create UX design specifications through collaborative exploration

commands.name: readiness
commands.description: Check implementation readiness of PRD, architecture, epics and stories

commands.name: party
commands.description: Multi-agent collaboration mode — all BMAD agents discuss your topic together

commands.name: document
commands.description: Document a brownfield project for AI context (full scan or deep dive)

commands.name: review-code
commands.description: Adversarial code review finding specific issues

commands.name: ci
commands.description: Scaffold CI/CD quality pipeline with test execution and quality gates

commands.name: quick
commands.description: Quick spec + dev flow for small changes (spec or dev mode)

commands.name: design-thinking
commands.description: Guide human-centered design using empathy-driven methodologies

commands.name: innovate
commands.description: Identify disruption opportunities and architect business model innovation

commands.name: solve
commands.description: Apply systematic problem-solving methodologies to complex challenges

commands.name: story-craft
commands.description: Craft compelling narratives using storytelling frameworks

commands.name: elicit
commands.description: Apply an advanced elicitation method to an artifact and save results

commands.name: context
commands.description: Generate an LLM-optimized project-context.md with implementation rules and patterns

commands.name: write-doc
commands.description: Write a document with the Tech Writer agent following documentation best practices

commands.name: mermaid
commands.description: Generate a Mermaid diagram with the Tech Writer agent

commands.name: readme
commands.description: Generate or update a README.md from project analysis

commands.name: changelog
commands.description: Generate changelog or release notes from git history and project changes

commands.name: api-docs
commands.description: Generate API documentation from source code (OpenAPI, JSDoc, etc.)

commands.name: jira
commands.description: Read epics and stories from Jira Cloud. Subcommands: config, epics [projectKey], stories [epicKey|projectKey], sync [projectKey]

commands.name: graph
commands.description: Show the graphify knowledge graph report (god nodes, communities, suggested questions)

commands.name: graph-query

commands.name: graph-bootstrap
commands.description: Install graphify and build the initial knowledge graph for this project

commands.name: graph-update
commands.description: Incrementally update the graphify knowledge graph (code-only, no LLM re-extraction)

commands.name: codeburn
commands.description: Show AI coding cost and token usage via Codeburn. Subcommands: today, week, month, report, models, claude, copilot, antigravity, opencode, compare, export, dashboard

commands.name: cost
commands.description: Alias for /codeburn report — show AI spend breakdown

commands.name: tokens
commands.description: Alias for /codeburn models — show per-model token usage

commands.name: help
commands.description: Describe what you want to do and the extension will recommend the best skills and agents for your task

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open the visual canvas to get started.
[$(preview) Open Visual Canvas](command:agileagentcanvas.openCanvas)
Or create a new project.
[$(add) New Project](command:agileagentcanvas.newProject)
[$(folder-opened) Load Existing Project](command:agileagentcanvas.loadProject)
[$(folder) Switch / Browse Project Folder](command:agileagentcanvas.switchProject)

agileagentcanvas.outputFolder.description: Name of the subfolder (inside your workspace) where Agile Agent Canvas stores artifacts. Change this to use a custom folder name instead of the default. You can also use the folder button in the canvas toolbar to browse for any folder or create a new one.

agileagentcanvas.autoSync.description: Automatically save canvas changes (drag, rename, status update, etc.) to the artifact files on disk. When disabled, changes remain in-memory until you use the Sync command. Useful if you want to batch edits before writing.

agileagentcanvas.showAICursor.description: Show a visual indicator on the canvas when the AI chat participant is working on an artifact. The AI cursor highlights the card being edited and shows a status label. Disable this if you find the cursor distracting.

agileagentcanvas.logLevel.description: Controls extension logging verbosity. Use info/warn/error for normal use; debug for troubleshooting.

agileagentcanvas.kanbanSkipConfirm.description: Skip per-drop confirmation modals on the Agentic Kanban board. When enabled, dragging a card between columns launches the workflow immediately instead of showing a Run/Skip prompt.

enumDescriptions: Automatically detect the best available provider (tries VS Code LM API first, then Antigravity, then OMP, then direct API key)
GitHub Copilot via VS Code Language Model API
OpenAI API (requires agileagentcanvas.apiKey and optionally agileagentcanvas.modelId)
Anthropic Claude API (requires agileagentcanvas.apiKey and optionally agileagentcanvas.modelId)
Google Gemini API (requires agileagentcanvas.apiKey and optionally agileagentcanvas.modelId)
Ollama local models (requires agileagentcanvas.baseUrl, e.g. http://localhost:11434)
Google Antigravity IDE — injects prompts into the Antigravity chat panel via native command
Oh My Pi (OMP) harness — writes the prompt to .omp/inbox.md or invokes omp.sendPrompt if the OMP VS Code extension is installed
agileagentcanvas.aiProvider.description: AI provider for Agile Agent Canvas. Use 'auto' to detect automatically.

agileagentcanvas.apiKey.description: API key for the selected AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini). Leave empty when using Copilot, Ollama, Antigravity, or OMP.

agileagentcanvas.modelId.description: Model ID to use. Defaults: OpenAI=gpt-4o, Anthropic=claude-sonnet-4-5, Gemini=gemini-2.0-flash, Ollama=llama3. Leave empty to use the default for your provider.

agileagentcanvas.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the AI API. Only needed for Ollama (e.g. http://localhost:11434) or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

agileagentcanvas.defaultTemperature.description: Default sampling temperature for LLM calls. 0 = deterministic, 2 = creative. Lower values improve structured output reliability. Used by OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama providers.

agileagentcanvas.defaultAgent.description: Default BMAD persona assigned when starting a new chat session. 'analyst' focuses on requirements and stories, 'pm' on strategic planning and roadmaps, 'architect' on technical design. You can switch agents per-session via /workflows.

enumDescriptions: Auto-detect the best available provider for the current host
GitHub Copilot chat panel (workbench.action.chat.open)
Anthropic Claude Code chat panel (claude.openChat) — opens Claude Code if installed, otherwise launches `claude` CLI in the terminal
Cursor chat panel (cursor.chat.open)
Windsurf chat panel (windsurf.openChat)
Google Antigravity chat panel (antigravity.sendPromptToAgentPanel)
Oh My Pi (OMP) harness — writes to .omp/inbox.md or invokes omp.sendPrompt
OpenAI Codex CLI — always launches in the integrated terminal
Google Gemini CLI — always launches in the integrated terminal
Aider — always launches in the integrated terminal
OpenCode CLI (sst/opencode) — always launches in the integrated terminal
Generic terminal: prints the prompt and lets the user run it manually
agileagentcanvas.chatProvider.description: Default AI chat provider for canvas actions (Refine, Enhance, Break Down, etc.). When set, all canvas-triggered actions route to this provider regardless of which IDE is hosting. Set per-action via the canvas header dropdown.

agileagentcanvas.chatProviderSelected.description: The chat provider most recently selected in the canvas dropdown. Persisted across sessions so the user doesn't need to re-pick. 'auto' = use the default from settings or auto-detect.

agileagentcanvas.jira.baseUrl.description: Your Jira Cloud base URL, e.g. https://mycompany.atlassian.net (no trailing slash). Required for Jira integration.

agileagentcanvas.jira.email.description: Your Atlassian account email address. Used for Jira API authentication.

agileagentcanvas.jira.apiToken.markdownDeprecationMessage: **Deprecated:** Storing the API token in plain-text settings is insecure. Use the command **Agile Agent Canvas: Set Jira API Token** (`agileagentcanvas.setJiraToken`) instead — it stores the token in the OS keychain. If a value is present here it will be migrated automatically on next use.
agileagentcanvas.jira.apiToken.description: [Deprecated — use setJiraToken command] Your Atlassian API token. Tokens expire after 1 year.

agileagentcanvas.jira.projectKey.description: Default Jira project key to use when none is specified (e.g. PROJ). You can override this per command.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.pythonPath.description: Path to the Python executable used to run graphify (python -m graphify). Change this if Python is not on your PATH, e.g. C:/Python311/python.exe or python3.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.backend.description: AI backend for graphify semantic extraction. Leave empty to use graphify's default. Passed as --backend flag to 'graphify extract'.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.autoBootstrapO.description: Prompt to bootstrap graphify when a workspace with BMAD artifacts but no knowledge graph is opened.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.autoUpdateOnSa.description: Automatically run 'graphify update' when source files change (debounced 5 s). Code-only — no LLM calls. Requires a graph to already exist.

agileagentcanvas.graphify.showCodebaseLa.description: Show the Codebase lane on the canvas when a graphify knowledge graph is present.

agileagentcanvas.codeburn.enabled.description: Enable Codeburn cost observability integration. When enabled, the extension can query codeburn for AI coding spend data.

agileagentcanvas.codeburn.path.description: Optional absolute path to the codeburn executable. Leave empty to auto-detect (global npm, local node_modules, or npx).

agileagentcanvas.headroom.enabled.description: Enable Headroom context compression. When enabled, chat messages are transparently compressed before reaching the AI provider, saving tokens. Requires the Headroom proxy to be running (npx headroom-ai proxy). Disable to opt out of compression.

agileagentcanvas.agenticKanban.enabled.description: Show the Agentic Kanban board — a Kanban-style workflow orchestration panel in the sidebar that lets you drag epics and stories across Backlog → Ready → In Progress → Review → Done columns. Each card shows execution state (queued, running, interrupted, terminal active) and concurrency locks. Disable to hide the board if you don't use agentic execution.

enumDescriptions: Auto-detect the best available terminal CLI for agentic execution
Anthropic Claude Code CLI — `claude` on PATH
OpenAI Codex CLI — `codex` on PATH
Google Gemini CLI — `gemini` on PATH
Aider — `aider` on PATH
OpenCode CLI (sst/opencode) — `opencode` on PATH
agileagentcanvas.agenticKanban.terminalP.description: Terminal CLI provider for agentic (headless) workflow execution. Used when a card is dragged in the Agentic Kanban without an active Copilot Chat session. 'auto' selects the first available CLI on PATH.

agileagentcanvas.trace.enabled.description: Enable execution tracing. When enabled, all agent tool calls and decisions are recorded to trace session files.

agileagentcanvas.trace.retentionDays.description: Number of days to retain trace session files. Older traces are automatically cleaned up.

agileagentcanvas.harness.enabled.description: Enable harness governance policies. Pre-flight checks block invalid artifact mutations; post-flight checks warn on advisory issues like placeholders or budget overruns.

agileagentcanvas.harness.sprintCapacity.description: Default sprint story-point capacity used by the token-budget harness policy to warn when an epic exceeds the team's capacity.

agileagentcanvas.agentTeam.enabled.description: Enable multi-agent teams via the Agent Coordination Protocol (ACP). When enabled, lane transitions can spawn coordinator+crafter+gate agent sessions instead of single-agent execution.

agileagentcanvas.yoloMode.description: Enable YOLO (autonomous) mode. When enabled, the AI may skip interactive checkpoints and proceed autonomously through workflow steps without waiting for user confirmation at each prompt.

agileagentcanvas.kanban.autoAdvance.description: When enabled, dropping a story into the In-Progress column on the Agentic Kanban runs the autonomous loop (implement → review → done), re-implementing on review failures until approved or the iteration cap is reached. When disabled, the card runs its single workflow and you move it between columns manually. Also toggleable from the Agentic Kanban toolbar.

agileagentcanvas.kanban.maxIterations.description: Maximum number of implement → review cycles the Agentic Kanban auto-advance loop will attempt before stopping a story without approval.

agileagentcanvas.kanban.wipLimits.description: Per-column work-in-progress (WIP) limits for the Agentic Kanban board. Set a max card count per column key (e.g. { "in-progress": 3, "review": 2 }). When exceeded, the column header turns red as a warning. Leave empty or omit columns for no limit.

agileagentcanvas.userCataloguePath.description: Absolute path to a folder containing user-managed skills and agents. Skills placed here are merged with the built-in catalogue; a skill with the same folder name overrides the built-in. Leave empty to use built-ins only. This is a global (user-level) setting shared across all workspaces.

agileagentcanvas.skillRepos.description: List of git repository URLs that contain skill folders to clone into your user catalogue. Each repo is cloned under <userCataloguePath>/_repos/<slug>/. Use the Skill Catalogue UI to manage repos.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Agile Agent Canvas
walkthroughs.description: Set up AI-assisted agile development with BMAD workflows and a code-aware knowledge graph — in minutes.

steps.title: Open the Agile Canvas
steps.description:
The canvas is your visual workspace for BMAD artifacts — epics, stories, vision, architecture, and more.

[Open Canvas](command:agileagentcanvas.openCanvas)

steps.title: Build a Codebase Knowledge Graph
steps.description:
graphify builds a semantic knowledge graph of your codebase so AI suggestions are automatically code-aware. Bootstrap once — it stays up to date automatically.

[Bootstrap graphify](command:agileagentcanvas.graphify.bootstrap)

steps.title: Start AI-Assisted Development
steps.description:
Use **@agileagentcanvas** in Copilot Chat to design epics, break down stories, review code, plan sprints — all grounded in your actual codebase and BMAD artifacts.

[Open Copilot Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open)

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run bundle && npm run compile-webview
scripts.pretest: node -e "const fs=require('fs'),path=require('path');const RE=/^\.test-traces-\d+$/;const SCRATCH=['cucumber-output.txt','cucumber-output.json','cucumber-full-output.txt','cucumber-remaining.json','full-output.txt','full-output-2.txt','remaining-failures.txt'];const root=process.cwd();let n=0;for(const e of fs.readdirSync(root)){if(RE.test(e)||SCRATCH.includes(e)){try{fs.rmSync(path.join(root,e),{recursive:true,force:true});n++;}catch{}}}console.log('[pretest] cleaned',n,'scratch entries');"
scripts.clean:scratch: node -e "const fs=require('fs'),path=require('path');const RE=/^\.test-traces-\d+$/;const SCRATCH=['cucumber-output.txt','cucumber-output.json','cucumber-full-output.txt','cucumber-remaining.json','full-output.txt','full-output-2.txt','remaining-failures.txt'];const root=process.cwd();let n=0;for(const e of fs.readdirSync(root)){if(RE.test(e)||SCRATCH.includes(e)){try{fs.rmSync(path.join(root,e),{recursive:true,force:true});n++;}catch{}}}console.log('cleaned',n,'scratch entries');"
scripts.test:coverage:ci: c8 --reporter=lcov --reporter=text cucumber-js --tags "not @wip" -f progress -f json:reports/cucumber-report.json && node check-coverage-thresholds.js
